name: Radial Menu
description: A circular selection UI that lets players choose tools, weapons, commands, or abilities by direction.
design_notes: Radial menus are strong for gamepad and quick directional selection, but weak when slices are too numerous or labels are unreadable. The design should define whether time slows, whether selection commits on release, and how mouse, touch, and keyboard users access the same choices.
design_purpose: Provide fast access to a small set of actions without opening a full menu.
Support directional muscle memory for weapons, tools, or commands.
Keep gameplay context visible while choosing from common options.
player_fantasy: The player feels able to quickly reach the right tool or command under pressure.

parameters.name: radial_slice_count
parameters.description: Number of selectable wedges in the menu.

parameters.name: selection_deadzone_radius
parameters.description: Center deadzone before a direction selects a slice.

parameters.name: commit_on_release
parameters.description: Whether releasing the menu button confirms the highlighted option.

parameters.name: radial_time_scale
parameters.description: Optional time scale while the menu is open.

parameters.name: label_min_arc_degrees
parameters.description: Minimum wedge angle before text labels remain visible.

edge_cases: Empty or locked slices should communicate why they cannot be selected.
Very small stick movement, mouse jitter, and touch release near boundaries need stable selection rules.
Opening the menu during reload, dialogue, pause, or multiplayer combat must define whether time changes.
Nested radial menus need a back/cancel path that does not accidentally commit a choice.
common_bugs: Release commits the previous slice because highlight updates after input release.
Stick deadzone differs from gameplay movement and causes accidental selections.
Time scale affects networked gameplay or UI animation unexpectedly.
Long localized labels overlap neighboring wedges.
balancing_notes: Time slowdown makes selection easier but reduces pressure; real-time wheels preserve risk.
A radial menu should usually contain few high-frequency choices rather than every item in the game.
accessibility_notes: Offer hold/toggle open behavior, remapping, larger labels, and non-radial list alternatives.
Avoid requiring precise analog direction for critical actions without confirmation or deadzone tuning.

implementation_notes.unity: Bind the UI to gameplay state through events or observable view models, not direct polling from presentation widgets; support UI Toolkit, uGUI, and input glyph updates from the active Input System scheme.
implementation_notes.godot: Drive Control nodes from a small view-model dictionary or signal payload, and keep input focus, localization, and scaling separate from gameplay state.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use UMG or CommonUI widgets fed by gameplay events, with focus navigation, platform glyphs, and replicated-state boundaries handled outside widget animation code.
implementation_notes.web: Render the UI from normalized game state, keep input mapping and localization data outside component logic, and avoid making animation completion the source of truth.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
example_games.note: Uses radial-style quick selection for powers and equipment contexts.

example_games.title: Red Dead Redemption 2
example_games.note: Uses radial wheels for weapons, items, and horse interactions.
